canonical_name: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
aliases: New Jersey faction of the Genovese crime family
description: The New Jersey faction of the Genovese crime family is a group of Italian-American mobsters controlling organized crime in New Jersey. It is divided into multiple crews involved in labor racketeering, illegal gambling, loansharking, and extortion. The faction has maintained a strong presence in Northern Jersey since the early Prohibition era. Notable members include Guarino 'Willie' Moretti, Gerardo 'Jerry' Catena, and Louis 'Bobby' Manna.

country_links.evidence_quote: Joseph Gatto was released in 2003, but he was indicted again in December 2004 for running Catalina Sports, an offshore wire room in Costa Rica.
country_links.context: The Genovese family's New Jersey faction operated an offshore wire room in Costa Rica for sports betting.

country_links.evidence_quote: Prisco controlled operations in Hudson County waterfronts cities of Bayonne and Jersey City; Monmouth County and Florida.
country_links.context: Angelo Prisco, a former faction boss, was imprisoned in Florida and controlled operations there.

country_links.evidence_quote: DeVita keeps strong family and criminal ties to Sicily.
country_links.context: The DeVita crew maintains strong family and criminal ties to Sicily, potentially recruiting Sicilian members.
